description: Controlled user memory QA comparison set (Experiment 3-12). Used to offline quantify the improvement in recalling user facts when 'contextualizing memory chunks before embedding/indexing' versus 'directly indexing raw conversation chunks'. Each chunk provides both text (raw conversation chunk) and context (context prefix, representing a 'situational anchor' summary generated by an LLM). gold is the correct memory chunk id for that query. The dataset deliberately includes several 'isolated fragments' (e.g., 'Okay, book this one'), which contain almost no retrievable signal out of context—this is exactly the pain point this chapter aims to illustrate.
note: This file is a controlled dataset for teaching purposes; in production, context is generated per chunk by an LLM and retrieved via neural embeddings (requires API). Here, deterministic BM25 lexical retrieval serves as an offline proxy without API, measuring the impact of concatenating the same context before and after on recall.

chunks.text:
User: Show me one-way flights from Shanghai to Seattle.
Assistant: Found Delta Air Lines DL588, departing October 12, one-way including tax $500.
chunks.context: This segment is from a flight booking conversation between a user and a travel assistant in October 2024, discussing a one-way flight from Shanghai to Seattle: Delta DL588, $500 including tax.

chunks.text:
User: Okay, book this one.
Assistant: Booked. An e-ticket will be sent shortly.
chunks.context: This segment follows the previous one; the user confirms booking that $500 Delta DL588 one-way ticket from Shanghai to Seattle.

chunks.text:
User: Any hotel recommendations in downtown Seattle?
Assistant: Recommend Hyatt Hotel, three nights from October 12 to 15, total $840.
chunks.context: This segment is from the same Seattle trip's hotel booking discussion: Hyatt Hotel, three nights October 12-15, $840.

chunks.text:
User: Okay, book it for me.
Assistant: Done, room locked.
chunks.context: This segment follows the previous one; the user confirms booking the Seattle Hyatt Hotel room for three nights ($840).

chunks.text:
Assistant: Your new checking account is open.
User: What's the number?
Assistant: Account number is 4429853327, routing number 123006800.
chunks.context: This segment is from a call where a user opens a checking account at First National Bank; the agent provides checking account number 4429853327 and routing number 123006800.

chunks.text:
User: Got it, use this one.
Assistant: Okay, set as default receiving account.
chunks.context: This segment follows the previous one; the user confirms using the First National Bank checking account 4429853327 as the default receiving account.

chunks.text:
User: By the way, is my passport still valid?
Assistant: Your passport expires on February 18, 2025. Please check before traveling.
chunks.context: This segment mentions that user Jessica's passport expires on February 18, 2025, related to her January Tokyo trip.

chunks.text:
Doctor: This time I'll adjust the dosage.
User: Okay, as you say.
chunks.context: This segment is from a follow-up consultation; the doctor adjusts the user's metformin dosage from 500 mg daily to 1000 mg daily.

chunks.text:
User: I don't eat cilantro and am allergic to peanuts.
Assistant: Recorded your dietary restrictions.
chunks.context: This segment records the user's dietary preferences and allergy information: no cilantro, allergic to peanuts.

chunks.text:
User: I want to get a gym membership.
Assistant: We have monthly and annual cards; the annual card is 1200 yuan.
chunks.context: This segment is from a gym membership consultation; annual card 1200 yuan.

chunks.text:
User: How much is car rental per day?
Assistant: Economy class $45 per day, including basic insurance.
chunks.context: This segment is from a car rental inquiry; economy class $45 per day.

chunks.text:
User: When should I renew my car insurance?
Assistant: Your policy expires on December 1st; you can renew one month in advance.
chunks.context: This segment is from a car insurance inquiry; policy expires December 1st.

queries.q: Which flight was the one I finally confirmed booking from Shanghai to Seattle?
Has my Seattle hotel been confirmed, and which hotel is it?
Which bank checking account did I set as the default for receiving payments?
What was the ticket price for that flight from Shanghai to Seattle?
What did the doctor adjust my metformin dosage to?
What are my dietary allergies and restrictions?
What is the total cost for three nights at the Hyatt Regency Seattle?
